description: An opening in a wall for light and air; a transparent portal to the outside. 'Wira' names what connects inside to outside—the opening that invites light and view.
sound_symbolism: The word opens view. 'Wi' widens in welcome, opening forming. Then 'ra' reaches outward, view extending beyond.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo wira' (the window). Allows light unlike 'ponu' (door) which allows passage. Related to seeing without crossing.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Windows as art—stained glass, flowing frames, the opening as decorative element.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: View without attachment—the window sees outside without needing to grasp it.
